frameworks.name: intersubject correlation
frameworks.one_line: use one brain's spatiotemporal pattern to model another brain's activity

frameworks.name: reverse correlation
frameworks.one_line: let brain signals pick optimal stimuli for each specialized cortical area

core_question: To what extent do individual brains share spatiotemporal activity patterns during free viewing of a naturalistic movie?
key_assumptions: free viewing of a popular movie elicits ecologically valid cortical activity
voxel-by-voxel cross-subject pattern similarity indexes shared processing
emotionally arousing scenes can drive widespread cortical activation
manipulations: naturalistic continuous movie stimulus vs. conventional controlled paradigms
across-subject pattern modeling rather than within-subject contrasts
open-ended reverse correlation from spontaneous BOLD signals
key_findings: striking voxel-by-voxel synchronization across subjects during natural vision
synchronization extends beyond primary/secondary sensory cortex into association cortices
a widespread component tracks emotionally arousing scenes
regionally selective components show stimulus tuning recovered by reverse correlation
interpretations: individual brains tend to 'tick collectively' under naturalistic input
reverse correlation can invert conventional analysis to discover preferred stimuli for specialized areas
